requirements.title: Native Rust unified LLM architecture boundary
requirements.summary: Define the Rust-owned unified LLM runtime as a layered adapter crate with provider specification types, provider utilities, a core client, and high-level API entry points, while preserving the existing Python modules only as oracle or compatibility assets.
acceptance_criteria: Normal Spark server and CLI LLM execution reaches Rust-owned unified LLM code without importing src.unified_llm provider clients.
The Rust crate exposes a provider adapter contract with name, complete, and stream operations plus optional initialize, close, and supports_tool_choice behavior where applicable.

The implementation separates shared types, provider utilities, core routing, and high-level orchestration so adapter authors do not need to modify the provider specification contract to add a provider.
Python unified_llm tests may still run as compatibility or oracle tests, but delivered runtime ownership is documented and exercised through Rust-facing tests.
requirements.notes: This requirement anchors the scope constraint that the Rust rewrite must replace normal runtime dependence on Python unified_llm provider clients.

requirements.title: Client construction and provider routing
requirements.summary: Implement Client construction from environment variables and explicit adapters, deterministic provider routing, default provider behavior, and module-level default client semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Client.from_env registers only providers whose required environment is present and selects the first registered provider as default.
Programmatic Client construction accepts explicit adapter instances and an explicit default provider.
A request with provider set is routed to that provider's adapter.
A request without provider is routed to default_provider.
A configuration error is raised when no provider is configured and a request does not specify a provider.
set_default_client overrides the lazily initialized environment-backed default client used by high-level entry points.
The client is safe to use concurrently and holds no mutable per-request state that can leak between requests.
requirements.notes: Provider resolution must never guess from model strings when no default provider exists.

requirements.title: Provider environment and model catalog
requirements.summary: Provide Rust-owned provider environment resolution and an advisory model catalog with lookup, listing, and latest-model selection behavior.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, and LiteLLM required and optional environment variables are resolved according to the spec.
GOOGLE_API_KEY is accepted as a fallback for GEMINI_API_KEY.
OpenRouter and LiteLLM are treated as first-class OpenAI-compatible providers and require explicit model selection rather than catalog defaults.
The model catalog includes provider, display name, context window, max output when known, tool support, vision support, reasoning support, costs when known, and aliases.
Unknown model strings are passed through to providers and are not rejected solely because they are absent from the catalog.
get_model_info, list_models, and get_latest_model return correct data and support provider and capability filtering.
requirements.notes: Catalog data should be updateable independently from adapter logic.

requirements.title: Middleware for complete and stream calls
requirements.summary: Support middleware that wraps non-streaming and streaming provider calls, can inspect or modify requests and responses, and observes stream events.
acceptance_criteria: Middleware executes in registration order on request entry and reverse registration order on response exit for complete calls.
Middleware can observe and transform complete responses without bypassing provider routing or error handling.
Streaming middleware wraps the event iterator and can observe or transform individual stream events.
Middleware ordering is covered by behavior tests for both complete and stream paths.
requirements.notes: Middleware is a cross-cutting extension point for logging, caching, budgets, rate limiting, prompt inspection, and circuit breakers.

requirements.title: Unified message and content model
requirements.summary: Implement the unified message, role, content part, and content data structures including multimodal inputs, tool calls, tool results, and thinking blocks.
acceptance_criteria: Message supports SYSTEM, USER, ASSISTANT, TOOL, and DEVELOPER roles.
Message convenience constructors create valid system, user, assistant, and tool result messages.
Message.text concatenates text from all text content parts and returns an empty string when no text exists.
ContentPart supports TEXT, IMAGE, AUDIO, DOCUMENT, TOOL_CALL, TOOL_RESULT, THINKING, REDACTED_THINKING, and provider-specific string kinds.
ToolCallData requires a stable ID, name, arguments as parsed JSON or raw string, and type.
ToolResultData links to a tool_call_id, carries structured or text content, records is_error, and can carry optional image data.
ThinkingData preserves text, signature, and redacted status for provider round-tripping.
Direction constraints for content kinds are validated or rejected with clear errors where the Rust API can enforce them.
requirements.notes: Provider-specific extension content kinds must not require changing the core enum.

requirements.title: Media content handling
requirements.summary: Translate image, audio, and document content through unified data structures, with full image support for URL, raw data, and local file path inputs.
acceptance_criteria: ImageData validates that exactly one of url or data is provided.
When raw image data has no media_type, image/png is used as the default.
Local image paths starting with slash, ./, or ~ are read, MIME type is inferred from extension, contents are base64 encoded, and the provider's inline data format is used.
OpenAI image URL and image data inputs are translated to Responses API input_image payloads, using data URIs for raw or local-file data.
Anthropic image URL and image data inputs are translated to image source url or base64 payloads.
Gemini image URL and image data inputs are translated to fileData or inlineData payloads.
Unsupported audio or document content for a provider is either translated correctly or rejected with a provider-specific warning or error without corrupting other content parts.
Behavior tests cover text plus image in the same user message.
requirements.notes: Image support is critical for coding-agent screenshot and diagram workflows.

requirements.title: Request response usage and metadata model
requirements.summary: Implement unified Request, Response, FinishReason, Usage, Warning, and RateLimitInfo types with accessors and aggregation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Request carries model, messages, optional provider, tools, tool_choice, response_format, temperature, top_p, max_tokens, stop_sequences, reasoning_effort, metadata, and provider_options.
Provider options are preserved as a provider-specific escape hatch and ignored by adapters that do not understand a key.
Response carries provider response ID, actual model, provider, assistant message, finish reason, usage, raw provider JSON, warnings, and optional rate limit metadata.
Response.text concatenates final text content, Response.tool_calls extracts tool calls, and Response.reasoning concatenates thinking text when available.
FinishReason preserves both unified reason and raw provider reason.
Provider finish reasons map to stop, length, tool_calls, content_filter, error, or other according to the spec tables.
Usage addition sums required integer fields and optional fields according to None-as-absent semantics.
RateLimitInfo is populated from provider response headers when available.
requirements.notes: Usage and finish reason normalization are shared by complete, stream, tools, structured output, and retries.

requirements.title: Native provider request translation
requirements.summary: Translate unified requests to each provider's native API, not a lowest-common-denominator compatibility protocol.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI native adapter sends requests to the Responses API /v1/responses and not Chat Completions.
Anthropic native adapter sends requests to the Messages API /v1/messages.
Gemini native adapter sends requests to the Gemini API generateContent endpoint.
System and developer messages are extracted or merged according to each provider convention.
All five roles are translated according to provider-specific role handling.
Tools and tool choice are translated according to the spec mapping for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini.
Generation parameters and response_format are translated to provider-native fields.
provider_options for the active provider are merged into the provider request body or headers without affecting other providers.
requirements.notes: This requirement preserves provider/model behavior while avoiding Python unified_llm clients in normal runtime.

requirements.title: Provider feature flags and beta headers
requirements.summary: Pass provider-specific feature flags through cleanly, including Anthropic beta headers and provider-specific body options for OpenAI and Gemini.
acceptance_criteria: provider_options.anthropic.beta_headers is joined into a comma-separated anthropic-beta request header.
Anthropic beta headers can enable thinking, interleaved thinking, token-efficient tools, prompt caching, and long-context features without changing the core Request type.
OpenAI built-in tool and feature options can be supplied through provider_options or equivalent typed options and appear in the Responses API body.

Gemini safety settings, grounding configuration, thinking configuration, and cached content references can be supplied through provider_options or equivalent typed options and appear in the request body.
Unsupported provider_options keys are ignored or surfaced as warnings without breaking portable requests.
requirements.notes: Escape hatch behavior is intentionally non-portable but must be explicit and observable.

requirements.title: Prompt caching support and accounting
requirements.summary: Support provider prompt caching behavior and map cache usage statistics into unified Usage fields.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI cache_read_tokens is populated from usage.input_tokens_details.cached_tokens when present.
Gemini cache_read_tokens is populated from usageMetadata.cachedContentTokenCount when present.
Anthropic request translation supports cache_control annotations on content blocks.
Anthropic automatic caching, when enabled, injects cache_control breakpoints on the system prompt, tool definitions, and stable conversation prefix using a documented heuristic.
Anthropic prompt-caching-2024-07-31 beta header is included automatically when cache_control is present.
Anthropic cache_read_tokens and cache_write_tokens are populated from provider usage fields.
provider_options.anthropic.auto_cache = false disables automatic Anthropic cache breakpoint injection.
A multi-turn agentic session test can observe cache_read_tokens for providers that report them.
requirements.notes: Anthropic caching is required because it is not automatic.

requirements.title: Reasoning and thinking token support
requirements.summary: Preserve reasoning controls, visible thinking blocks, signatures, and reasoning token accounting across providers.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI reasoning_effort maps to Responses API reasoning.effort for GPT-5+ reasoning models.
OpenAI reasoning_tokens is populated from usage.output_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens.
Anthropic extended thinking options enable thinking blocks through provider_options and beta headers.
Anthropic thinking and redacted_thinking content blocks are returned as THINKING or REDACTED_THINKING content parts.
Anthropic thinking signatures and redacted payloads are round-tripped verbatim in subsequent assistant messages.
Anthropic reasoning_tokens is estimated from thinking blocks when exact provider counts are unavailable.
Gemini thinkingConfig can be passed through and usageMetadata.thoughtsTokenCount maps to reasoning_tokens.
Usage reports reasoning_tokens separately from output_tokens.
requirements.notes: Thinking signatures are only portable for same-provider, same-model continuation.

requirements.title: Provider response translation
requirements.summary: Normalize provider complete responses into the unified Response, Message, ContentPart, FinishReason, Usage, raw, warning, and rate-limit model.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI Responses API output items are parsed into text, tool call, and usage fields, including reasoning token details.
Anthropic content blocks are parsed into text, tool_use, thinking, redacted_thinking, and usage fields.
Gemini candidates parts are parsed into text, functionCall, thought parts when available, finish reason, and usageMetadata.
Gemini tool-call responses generate stable synthetic tool call IDs and preserve mappings needed for follow-up tool results.
Provider raw response JSON is preserved in Response.raw for debugging.
Provider rate limit headers are parsed into RateLimitInfo when present.
Finish reason mapping tests cover stop, length, tool_calls, content_filter, error, and other outcomes.
requirements.notes: Response translation must preserve observable behavior used by existing adapters and compatibility tests.

requirements.title: Error taxonomy and provider error translation
requirements.summary: Implement the unified error hierarchy, provider error fields, retryability classification, HTTP status mapping, Gemini gRPC mapping, message-based classification, and rate-limit metadata.
acceptance_criteria: All errors inherit from SDKError and expose message and optional cause.
ProviderError exposes provider, status_code, error_code, retryable, retry_after, and raw response fields.
HTTP statuses 400, 401, 403, 404, 408, 413, 422, 429, and 500-599 map to the specified error types and retryable flags.
Gemini gRPC status codes map to the specified unified error types.
Ambiguous error bodies are classified by message signals for not found, authentication, context length, content filter, and safety cases.
Retry-After is parsed from provider headers and stored on rate limit errors.
Unknown provider errors default to retryable.
Provider error translation preserves raw provider error bodies for debugging.
requirements.notes: RequestTimeoutError is non-retryable by default unless application retry policy opts in.

requirements.title: Retry policy and backoff behavior
requirements.summary: Provide high-level retry behavior with exponential backoff, jitter, Retry-After handling, per-call retry scope, and low-level no-retry semantics.
acceptance_criteria: RetryPolicy supports max_retries, base_delay, max_delay, backoff_multiplier, jitter, and on_retry callback.
Backoff delay uses min(base_delay * backoff_multiplier^attempt, max_delay).
Jitter multiplies delay by a random factor between 0.5 and 1.5 when enabled.
Retry-After overrides calculated backoff when present and not greater than max_delay.
A Retry-After greater than max_delay prevents retry and raises the original error with retry_after set.
max_retries = 0 disables high-level automatic retries.
Low-level Client.complete and Client.stream do not retry automatically.
High-level generate retries individual LLM calls, not entire multi-step operations.
Streaming only retries the initial connection and does not retry after partial data has been delivered.
generate_object does not retry schema validation failures.
requirements.notes: Retry semantics must preserve existing error and streaming behavior.

requirements.title: Unified streaming event model
requirements.summary: Normalize provider streaming protocols into StreamEvent values with start/delta/end lifecycle events, final response accumulation, and error propagation.
acceptance_criteria: StreamEvent supports type, text delta and text_id fields, reasoning deltas, tool call data, finish reason, usage, full response, error, and raw provider event payloads.
Client.stream returns an async iterator immediately and yields StreamEvent values as provider data arrives.
Every successful stream terminates with FINISH containing finish_reason, usage, and the complete accumulated Response.
Text, reasoning, and tool call events follow a start/delta/end lifecycle with stable IDs.
Consumers that filter TEXT_DELTA can concatenate deltas to the same text returned by the final response.
Stream errors after partial delivery surface as stream error events or stream errors without silently retrying.
Stream resources can be explicitly closed to avoid leaking provider connections.
requirements.notes: The start/delta/end pattern is required for multiple concurrent content segments and tool calls.

requirements.title: Provider streaming translation
requirements.summary: Implement SSE and provider-specific streaming parsers for OpenAI Responses API, Anthropic Messages API, Gemini streaming chunks, and OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions.
acceptance_criteria: The SSE parser handles event lines, multi-line data lines, retry lines, comment lines, and blank event boundaries.

OpenAI Responses streaming events map response.created to STREAM_START, output_text.delta to TEXT_DELTA, function_call_arguments.delta to TOOL_CALL_DELTA, output_item.done to text or tool-call end events, and response.completed to FINISH.
Anthropic streaming events map message_start, content_block_start, content_block_delta, content_block_stop, message_delta, and message_stop to unified stream events.
Gemini SSE or JSON chunks map text parts, functionCall parts, finish reasons, and final usage to unified stream events.
Gemini function calls emitted as complete objects produce TOOL_CALL_START and TOOL_CALL_END.
OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions streaming handles delta content, delta tool_calls, usage chunks, and data: [DONE].
Final streaming usage includes reasoning and cache token fields when providers report them.
requirements.notes: OpenAI native streaming must use the Responses API event vocabulary.

requirements.title: High-level generate and stream APIs
requirements.summary: Provide high-level non-streaming and streaming generation APIs that standardize prompts, call the core client, execute tool loops, aggregate usage, and expose step results.
acceptance_criteria: generate accepts either prompt or messages, but rejects calls that provide both.
prompt is converted to a single user message and system is prepended as a system message.
generate returns GenerateResult with text, reasoning, tool_calls, tool_results, finish_reason, usage, total_usage, steps, response, and optional output.
usage is the final step usage and total_usage aggregates all steps.
stream accepts the same generation parameters and yields a StreamResult over unified events.
StreamResult.response returns the accumulated response after stream completion.
StreamResult.text_stream yields only text deltas.
StreamResult.partial_response exposes current accumulated state while streaming.
StreamAccumulator can rebuild a complete Response from stream events.
requirements.notes: Low-level client calls stay no-retry; high-level APIs apply retry behavior.

requirements.title: Cancellation and timeout handling
requirements.summary: Support cooperative cancellation and timeout scopes for high-level operations and provider adapters.
acceptance_criteria: generate accepts an abort signal and raises AbortError when the signal is triggered before completion.
stream accepts an abort signal, closes the underlying provider connection, and raises or emits AbortError when cancelled.
High-level operations accept total timeout and per-step timeout configuration.
Provider adapters support connect, request, and stream_read timeout scopes with default values of 10 seconds, 120 seconds, and 30 seconds respectively.
Timeout errors use RequestTimeoutError and follow retryability rules from the error taxonomy.
Timeout behavior is tested for complete and stream paths without depending on provider live services.
requirements.notes: Adapters may expose async-native APIs and sync wrappers where appropriate, but behavior must match the spec.

requirements.title: Tool definitions choices and passive tool calls
requirements.summary: Implement tool definitions, tool execute handler contracts, ToolChoice translation, ToolCall and ToolResult extraction, and passive tool behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tool names validate as [a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]* with maximum length 64.
Tool parameter schemas require a JSON Schema object at the root.
Tool execute handlers accept parsed JSON arguments and return string, object, list, or other JSON-serializable values.
Tool execute handler errors become tool error results when used in high-level tool loops.
Recognized injected context values include messages, abort_signal, and tool_call_id where the language interface supports injection.
ToolChoice supports auto, none, required, and named modes, with tool_name required for named mode.
ToolChoice maps to provider-specific values according to the spec, including omitting Anthropic tools entirely for none mode.
Unsupported tool choice modes raise UnsupportedToolChoiceError.
Passive tools without execute handlers return tool calls to the caller without automatic execution.
requirements.notes: ToolCall preserves raw_arguments before parsing for debugging and repair.

requirements.title: Automatic tool execution loop
requirements.summary: Implement high-level active tool execution loops with round limits, concurrent execution, ordered batched results, validation or repair, and streaming step boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: generate with active tools automatically executes returned tool calls and continues until no tool calls remain, finish_reason is not tool_calls, max_tool_rounds is reached, or stop_when returns true.
max_tool_rounds = 1 permits the initial call, one tool execution batch, and one continuation call.
max_tool_rounds = 0 disables automatic tool execution and returns tool calls to the caller.
Multiple tool calls returned in one model response are executed concurrently.
All tool results from one response are sent back together in a single continuation request.
Tool result ordering matches the original tool call ordering even when execution completes out of order.
Partial tool execution failures are sent as is_error = true results without aborting successful sibling calls.
Unknown tool calls produce error tool results instead of exceptions.
Tool argument strings are parsed as JSON and optionally validated against the tool schema before handler execution.
Configured repair behavior is attempted when validation fails; otherwise an error tool result is sent.
Streaming with active tools emits tool call events as they form and a step_finish event between model steps.
requirements.notes: Parallel execution and single continuation batching are required for coding-agent correctness.

requirements.title: Structured output generation
requirements.summary: Implement response formats, generate_object, stream_object, provider-specific structured-output strategies, validation, and failure behavior.
acceptance_criteria: ResponseFormat supports text, json, and json_schema with strict mode and required schema for json_schema.
generate_object sends native strict JSON Schema response_format to OpenAI where supported.
generate_object sends Gemini responseMimeType application/json and responseSchema where supported.
generate_object supports Anthropic structured output through schema instructions or tool-based extraction.
generate_object parses and validates final output against the requested schema and returns it in GenerateResult.output.
NoObjectGeneratedError is raised when parsing or validation fails.
stream_object incrementally parses JSON text from streaming events and yields partial object updates.
stream_object.object returns the final validated object after stream completion.
requirements.notes: Schema validation failures are model-output failures and are not retried by default.

requirements.title: OpenAI-compatible provider adapters
requirements.summary: Provide an OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions adapter for third-party endpoints and named OpenRouter and LiteLLM wrappers with correct environment and model behavior.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAICompatibleAdapter uses /v1/chat/completions and not /v1/responses.
OpenAICompatibleAdapter translates unified messages, tools, tool choice, responses, usage, errors, and Chat Completions streams into unified types.
The compatible adapter documents and exposes that reasoning tokens, built-in Responses API tools, and Responses-only features are unsupported.
OpenRouterAdapter defaults to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 and requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY unless explicitly configured otherwise.
OpenRouter forwards OPENROUTER_HTTP_REFERER and OPENROUTER_TITLE when configured.
LiteLLMAdapter requires LITELLM_BASE_URL and accepts optional LITELLM_API_KEY.
OpenRouter and LiteLLM require explicit model selection rather than using provider catalog defaults.
requirements.notes: This path exists for compatibility endpoints and must remain distinct from native OpenAI.

requirements.title: Cross-provider parity validation
requirements.summary: Validate the Rust runtime against a cross-provider matrix for text, streaming, media, tools, structured output, reasoning, errors, usage, caching, and provider options.
acceptance_criteria: Parity tests cover simple text generation for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini using mocked or recorded provider interfaces where live keys are unavailable.
Parity tests cover streaming text generation and assert text deltas concatenate to the final response text.
Parity tests cover image URL and base64 image inputs for each native provider.
Parity tests cover single tool calls, multiple parallel tool calls, three-or-more-round tool loops, and streaming with tool calls.
Parity tests cover generate_object structured output for each native provider strategy.
Parity tests cover reasoning or thinking token reporting for each provider.
Parity tests cover invalid API key or authentication errors and rate limit errors.
Parity tests cover accurate usage token mapping and provider-specific options pass-through.
A real-key smoke test plan exists for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini and is gated so it does not fail ordinary local test runs without credentials.
requirements.notes: Tests should assert observable behavior rather than source strings or prompt text.

requirements.title: Migration completion and documentation
requirements.summary: Record Rust ownership, Python compatibility status, validation evidence, and operational guidance for the unified LLM runtime migration.
acceptance_criteria: docs/rust-rewrite-migration.md identifies the unified LLM runtime path as Rust-owned for normal Spark server and CLI execution.
The migration document states which Python unified_llm tests remain oracle or historical compatibility tests.
The migration document records any retained Python unified_llm surfaces that are not part of normal runtime execution.

Validation evidence references the Rust tests and compatibility tests that cover provider routing, request and response translation, streaming, errors, retry, usage, tools, structured output, and caching.
Any unsupported or intentionally deferred provider capabilities are documented as explicit non-goals or future work, not as silent gaps.
The full repository test suite passes with uv run pytest -q before the implementation is reported complete.
requirements.notes: This closes the spec by tying delivered behavior to the Rust rewrite migration record.
